content.full_text:
Fern Porrrrcar PnacrrcEs CouvussroN
428 J Street. Suite 620. Sacramento, CA 95914_2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
April2l,2003
Jay A. Lembach
303 So. Ditmar Street
Oceanside, CA92O54
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A.-03-048
Dear Mr. Lembach:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ..Act,)..
QUESTTON
Do you have an economic interest in a nonprofit corporation that you are
forming?
CONCLUSION
You will not have an economic interest in this entity provided you do not receive
income from it. If you do not have an economic interest inthis entity, it cannot tigger a
conflict of interest for you.
FACTS
You are the finance manager for the City of Encinitas. You are in the process of
forming a nonprofit corporation to acquire, develop, and manage affordable housing in
your local area. As part of that venture, you are proposing to acquire or develop low-
income housing within the City of Encinitas. Any such pioposal will involve certain
subsidies by the city to the nonprofit corporation in support of the project.
' Gove.nment Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
r€=


File No. 4-03-048
Page No. 2
The nonprofit corporation will be duly organized,with a stated purpose of
developing affordable housing, and will have an independent board of directors. you
will act as chief executive officer. A development fee earned on each project ranges up
to SYo of the eligible_costs on acquisitionprdects and up to llyoon devellpment
projects, and would be paid to the nonprofit corporation from the project budget. you
will have partners in anyproposed venture, and one of those individuals wifl [e the
pimaty contact with city staffregarding development and entitlement issues. As part of
the process, you will have occasion to meet with certain city officials and may make
presentations before the planning commission and city council. you intend to trtty
disclose your economic interests in any project and to meet, confer or speak only in your
capacity as a private citizen.
You are employed by the City of Encinitas as the finance manage\and in that
capacity you also act as the city treasurer and investment officer. You iupervise four
finance technicians within the finance department and work directly with^the director of
finance. You do not have any decision-making authority outside olaccounting and
financial reporting matters. You also do not have any involvement or decision-making
authority in any aspects of the city's housing programs, nor do you make any
recorrmendations regarding how housing funds should be spent or allocated. fhose
recommendations come directly from the community development director to the city
manager:' all decisions are ultimately made by the city council.
You do not have access to any confidential or privileged information regarding
the city's housing programs. In order to avoid even the appearance of a confliJ, the clty
manager and you have agreed that it would be appropriate for you to have your director
handle any housing/finance issues that could be related in any way to potential
transactions between yourself and the city.
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials ..perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused ty ttreir own financial interests
or the financial interests ofpersons who have supported them.,, (Section gl00l(b).)
Specifically, section 87100 prohibits any public oflicial from makin g, participating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence aldvernmental
decision in which the official has a financial interest.
A public official has a "financial interest" in a govemmental decision within the
meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable thatihe govemmental decision will
have a material financial effect on one or more of the public official's economic interests.
(Section 87103l' Regulation 1 8700(a).) The Commission has adopted a standard analysis
for deciding whether an individual has a disqualifting conflict oflnterest in a given


File No. 4-03-048
Page No. 3
govemmental decision.2 (Regulation 18700(bX1)-(8).) However, your question concerns
only the identification of your economic interests, step three of this analysis.
What are your economic interests?
Section 87103 provides that apublic official has a "financial interes t, in a
govemmental decision "if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the
official, a member of his or her immediate farrriTy," or on any of tn" official,s economic
interests, described as follows:
o 
d public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she
has a direct or indirect investrnent 3 of $2,000 or more (section 87103(a);
regulation 18703.1(a)); or in which he or she is a director, officer, partner, trusteg
employee, or holds any position of management (section 87103(Olregulation
18703.1(b));
' 
A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a
direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more (section SZf Of 6l; regulation
18703.2);
o 
d public official has an economic interest in any source of income, including
promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the
decision (section 37 I 03 (c); regulation 1 3703.3);
o 
d public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if
the gifts aggregate to $340 or more within 12 months priorio the decision (section
871 03(e); regulation 187 03.4);
' 
A public official has an economic interest in his or her personal finances,
including those of his or her immediate family -- this is ttre "personal financial
effects" rule (section 87103; regulation Ig703.5).
"Income" is "a pa5rment received" and includes, inpartr.,any salary, wage,
advance...." (Section 82030(a), enclosed.) However, income does not include:
2 This analysis is summarized in the Commission document entitled, "Can I Vote? Conflict-of-
Interest Overview" (enclosed).
' An indirect inveshnent or interest means any investment or interest owned by the spouse of an
official or by a member of the official's immediate family, by an agent on behalf of a putti" oin"iul, or by
a business entity or trust in which the official, the officiai's immediate family, or their agents own directly,
indirectly, or beneficially a 10-percent interest or greater. (Section 87103.) "Immediate family,,is defined
at section 82029 as an offrcial's spouse and dependent children.


File No. ,4.-03-048
Page No. 4
;:"x"H;ffif "j?i['i#:i'#t"#,:l;:Tffi:liE**
and reimbursement for travel expenses and per diem
received from a bona fide nonprofit entity exempt from
taxation under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue
Code." (Section 82030(b)e)) (Footnote added.)
Under section 82030(b), "reimbursement for travel expenses and per diern
received from a bona fide nonprofit entity" are excluded fromthe definiti,on of ..income,,,
provided the nonprofit entity is exempt from taxation under section 501(c)(3) of the
Internal Revenue Code. But, salary and other payments, including medicai and dental
benefits, received by a public official from a nonprofit entity "o6titot" income under the
AcL As a result, you will have an economic interest in the nonprofit corporation you are
forming if you receive such income from this entity.
Assuming you have an economic interest in the nonprofit, you would have to
consider whether aparticular decision would have a material financial effect on the
nonprofit under the Commission's conflict of interest rules. This analysis requires that
you consider each decision which comes before you as finance manager.
You have mentioned that your director will handle any housin glfinanceissues.
This plan may be a viable way of avoiding a conflict of interest arising from the
nonprofit. However, without specific fact3 regarding each decision, w=e cannot
conclusively tell you that this method will allow you to participate in every decision
which comes before you as finance manager.
Please note that if you do not receive income from the nonprofit entity,you will
not have an economic interest here that would trigger a conflict of interest foi you.
- We point out that you have provided information regarding only your government
salarys and your involvement with the nonprofit you are forming. Forpurposes of this
letter, we assume that you have no other potential economic interests.6
Your letter may also raise questions under other provisions of law, such as
Govemment Code section 1090. We do not provide advice conceming laws other than
the Act. Please consult with your agency's attorney or the Attorney General,s office
regarding other laws thatmay pertain to your situation.
4 "'Salary' from a state, local, or federal government agency means any and all payments made by
a goveflrment agency to a public official, or accrued to the benefit of a public official, as consideration for
the public official's services to the government agency. Such paymentr i.rrlod" *ug.r, fees paid to public
officials as 'consultants'... pension benefits, health and other insurance coverage, rights to compensated
vacation and leave time, free or discounted transportation, payment or indemniicatiJn of legal defense
costs, and similar benefits." (Regulation 18232(a).)
5 Pursuant to the "government salary exception" of section 82l3},you do not have an economic
interest in the government agency for which you work.
" Please bear in mind that this letter is based on the facts you have presented to us. The
Cornmission does not act as a finder of fact inproviding advice. (in re Oglisby (1975) I FppC Ops. 71.)


File No. 4-03-048
Page No. 5
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
By:
atalie
Enclosure
NB je
I:\Adviceltrs\03-048
Counsel, Legal Division

QUESTION: Do you have an economic interest in a nonprofit corporation that you are
forming?

CONCLUSION: You will not have an economic interest in this entity provided you do not receive
income from it. If you do not have an economic interest inthis entity, it cannot tigger a
conflict of interest for you.
